page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile for this instance. In raw CPU compute tests, the stress-ng single-core performance is categorized in the bottom 10%, and its multi-core performance falls in the bottom 25%. However, the instance demonstrates average performance across a wide range of standard workloads. Geekbench scores, including single-core (1315.0) and multi-core (5025.0) metrics, align with the middle 50% of tested servers. Similarly, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks fall within average ranges. Memory bandwidth shows a notable tradeoff: while cached small-block read performance is average at 395.92 GB/sec, uncached large-block read and write speeds are weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c6a.2xlarge offers a dedicated AMD EPYC hardware profile that balances compute resources with a lower memory-to-CPU ratio. This resource density makes it a viable option for workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without massive memory footprints. Given the average performance in Redis SET operations (7.44 million operations per second), static web serving throughput (6.78 GB/sec), and Passmark database operations, this server is suited for moderate web serving, basic database operations, text processing, and general-purpose utility tasks that do not demand high-bandwidth uncached memory access.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate a performance tradeoff: while raw stress-ng CPU metrics are weak, the instance achieves average scores in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and static web serving. Memory performance is average for cached small blocks but weak for uncached large-block operations. Qualitatively, this instance offers a cost-effective dedicated CPU profile for workloads with moderate memory requirements. It is best suited for web serving, standard database operations, and general-purpose utility tasks.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance offering balanced performance for web serving, database operations, and general-purpose workloads.

Configured with 16.0 GB of RAM and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average performance in web serving, software compilation, and database operations
